channel.title: Andrea Galmán on DICE Lab: Diversity In Changing Environments
channel.description: Recent content in Andrea Galmán on DICE Lab: Diversity In Changing Environments

item.title: Inducibility of chemical defences in young oak trees is stronger in species with high elevational ranges
item.description: Abstract Elevational gradients have been highly useful for understanding the underlying forces driving variation in plant traits and plant-insect herbivore interactions.

item.title: Interspecific variation in leaf functional and defensive traits in oak species and its underlying climatic drivers
item.description: Abstract Plants exhibit a diverse set of functional traits and ecological strategies which reflect an adaptation process to the biotic and abiotic components of the environment.
